title: Issuance of Order of Preventive Suspension. Pending investigation of
content: 6 the recruitment violation or disciplinary action complaint, the erring LMA, foreign 7 principal/employer, or seafarer may be suspended by the Department for a period not 8 exceeding ninety (90) calendar days, on the following grounds: 9 10 a. For the LMA 11 12 1. There exists reasonable ground to believe that the continued operation of the 13 LMA will lead to further violation or exploitation of workers being recruited or 14 deployed, or will adversely affect friendly relations with any country, or will 15 prejudice national interest; and 16 17 2. There is a prima facie evidence of a case for violation of the DMW Rules and 18 Regulations pertaining to a serious or less serious offense. 19 20 b. For a foreign principal/employer 21 22 1. There exists reasonable ground to believe that the continued deployment to the 23 principal/employer will result in further violation or exploitation of OFWs; and 24 25 2. There is a prima facie evidence of a case for violation of the DMW Rules and 26 Regulations. 27 28 c. For a seafarer 29 30 There is a prima facie evidence of a case for violation of the DMW Rules and 31 Regulations pertaining to a serious offense and there is reasonable ground to 32 believe that the continued deployment of the worker shall be harmful to the life of 33 other overseas workers, public health, morality, human dignity, or the dignity of the 34 Republic of the Philippines. 35 36

metadata.source_file: [NOTICE TO PUBLIC] CLEAN DRAFT OF THE DMW SEABASED RULES.pdf
